title: Amiss scanner report envelope

RepoPathText.description: A repository-relative path whose raw bytes are valid UTF-8, in slash-separated segments.

RepoPathBytes.description: A path the report cannot hold as text: the raw bytes as lowercase hex. The pattern enforces the byte grammar pair-aligned (no NUL or backslash byte, no leading, doubled, or trailing slash, no dot or dot-dot segment). That the bytes do not decode as UTF-8 is the writer's canonical-form law, which no pattern can state.

RepositoryIdentity.description: The declared forge identity. The host is an opaque claim the engine never resolves, normalizes, or judges; anything except a slash, which would make the host/owner/name triple ambiguous. The caller owns canonical spelling: controls bind and links are recognized by exact bytes, so one host must have one spelling. The owner is one or more slash-joined segments; nested segments spell a GitLab group path. Evaluation repositories emit a slashed owner only when their forge is gitlab or null; execution constraints may independently name any open action-repository identity.

entries.description: Complete logical stage-zero index, sorted by raw path bytes.

reasons.description: All applicable reasons in the item-enum order.

files.description: All build lockfiles, sorted by path UTF-8 bytes; paths are unique.

runtime_files.description: Complete action-supplied runtime closure, sorted by path UTF-8 bytes; paths are unique.

artifacts.description: Sorted by platform UTF-8 bytes; each listed platform occurs at most once.

required_status_name.description: Expected emitted provider context only; this field does not identify the source check and cannot authorize a stable required deployment without authenticated provider request acquisition.

forge.description: The same-repository URL dialect this run recognized, or null when none applied: no declared identity, or an unknown host without an explicit dialect. Under null every absolute forge link remains external and the same_repository counter stays zero. github and gitea appear only with a single-segment owner.

reasons.description: All applicable reasons in the item-enum order.
All applicable reasons in the item-enum order.

base.description: Every non-primary plausible base occurrence, sorted by observation_id UTF-8 bytes.

candidate.description: Every non-primary plausible candidate occurrence, sorted by observation_id UTF-8 bytes.

sources.description: Sorted by digest UTF-8 bytes; digest keys are unique and multiplicity preserves duplicate evidence.

observation_ids.description: Every distinct contributing base/candidate observation ID, sorted by UTF-8 bytes.

same_repository.description: References recognized as same-repository by the run's dialect. The dialect is the run-level evaluation forge value, so the counter carries no per-forge split: only one dialect can apply to a run.

observations.description: Sorted by candidate observation_id, falling back to base observation_id.

findings.description: Exactly one aggregate per finding_key, sorted by finding_key then kind.

errors.description: Sorted by the complete typed error key with null before non-null values.

description: The rolling pre-1.0 report contract. Public wire contracts remain intentionally unversioned while compatibility is experimental.
